title: labichea teretifolia subsp. teretifolia
intro: Labichea teretifolia subsp. teretifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the "stinkwood" due to its strong and unpleasant odor. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective against a range of health issues. The health benefits of Labichea teretifolia subsp. teretifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and treat various infections. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
